posts.name: Brian S t o t t e r
posts.cooked:
Was texting with David Dyroff about buying some Hofgut Falkenstein, who mentioned a particular retailer had the “speed laser find Herb” (spatlese feinherb).

I’m sure this must happen to many of you, what have been some of your favorite autocorrects?
posts.display_username: Brian S t o t t e r

posts.name: Ken Lobel
posts.cooked: Lunch-Bagels

posts.name: Brian S t o t t e r
posts.cooked:
Lunch-Bagels

Tasty.


I’m currently in the mood for some spotless (spatlese).
posts.display_username: Brian S t o t t e r

Morgan

Ru Yi
Coat the brewery

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked:
To be fully accurate, “Speed laser find Herb” from the OP was actually a voice-dictation fail rather than an auto-correct fail.

But yesterday auto-correct wanted me to call everyone’s favorite second-growth Pauillac “Chateau Pichon-Laplander.”

posts.name: Patrick T a y l o r
posts.cooked:
To be fully accurate, “Speed laser find Herb” from the OP was actually a voice-dictation fail rather than an auto-correct fail.

But yesterday auto-correct wanted me to call everyone’s favorite second-growth Pauillac “Chateau Pichon-Laplander.”

Chateau Pichon-Laplander. Notes of fir tree, lutefisk, and soul-draining cold. No rating.
posts.display_username: Patrick T a y l o r

What, no scent of reindeer jerky?

posts.name: Andrew Hamilton
posts.cooked: Just recently I tried to write Musigny in a Instagram post and it auto-corrected to Misogyny. Glad I caught that one.

posts.name: Jay Miller
posts.cooked: I fondly recall the only Sherry Equipo Navazos ever released from Florida, their famous Manzanilla Pasadena.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: My autocorrect is pretty good; predicts echezeaux, musigny, Angerville (actually corrects anger to Angerville lol)

posts.name: Gary Schulte
posts.cooked:
Lunch-Bagels

That came from Lynch Bages ? Wow.

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked:
My autocorrect is pretty good; predicts echezeaux, musigny, Angerville (actually corrects anger to Angerville lol)

Mine finally predicts certain words with the correct diacritics, including spätlese and Rhône (my phone also has Côte and Rôtie, but my iPad is still learning those), but it still won’t stop capitalizing grape varieties (other than riesling, on which I’ve finally trained it) and words like spätlese (on which I had to correct it twice for this post).

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Best was when I started covering Champagne: “mousse” was being corrected to “mouse”. I only noticed a few days before my first big article was due to publish…

posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked: I’ve had so many autocorrects on wine. But the AI learns maybe a little too well. If I type in the letter F, the autocorrect defaults to Falkenstein. Hu to Huet.
